issues.problem: Two sections share the prefix '10_pause1' ('10_pause1' and '10_pause1_silence') with identical narration. Not a math error, but a structural duplicate that may cause downstream tooling to treat the silence section as the same slide. Worth a one-line clarification but does not affect mathematical correctness.

issues.note: All stated math is correct: A'(x)=8-6x^2=0 gives x=sqrt(4/3); perimeter-40 rectangle yields 10x10 square; implicit dy/dx=-x/y on circle of radius 5 gives horizontal tangents at (0,+/-5) and vertical at (+/-5,0). Recap claim of 'six takeaways' matches the six items listed.

issues.location: section 08 (inscribed rectangle worked example) and section 09 (verify step)
issues.problem: The natural feasibility interval for the inscribed-rectangle problem is x in [0,2] (since 4 - x^2 >= 0 and x >= 0). The narration never explicitly narrates checking endpoints x=0 and x=2 against the interior critical point. Given Unit 5's emphasis on the closed-interval candidates test, walking through one boundary check on-screen would close this gap.

issues.location: section 06 (sketch f from f' worked example)
issues.problem: The AP trap 'misreading a graph of f'(x) as a graph of f(x)' is listed in ap_traps_covered but does not appear in the section-06 narration where the student is most likely to fall into it. Adding one sentence inside the worked example narration ('remember: the graph you are looking at is f', not f') would convert a passive list item into an active warning.

The hook frames a 350 mL can but the outline never solves it or narrates the mL -> cm^3 unit conversion. Since it is hook framing only, this is acceptable, but a follow-up module or assignment problem should make the conversion explicit so students do not internalize unit-skipping.

'feasibility check' appears in vocabulary but never enters narration as a labeled step in the 5-step method. Either fold it into the 5-step framework slide (07) or drop it from vocab to avoid a defined term the student never sees used.

Outline cites 'Unit 5b — FUN 5.8 to FUN 5.12'. The reference text calls it simply 'Unit 5 — Analytical Applications of Differentiation (topics 5.8-5.12)'. The '5b' suffix is an internal GIIS navigational split (presumably because Module 5 covered 5.1-5.7) and is not a College Board label. Acceptable as internal sequencing but not a citation from the source.

issues.note: All topic codes FUN 5.8 through FUN 5.12 are present in the reference. Named theorems/methods referenced (MVT, EVT, second derivative test, closed-interval candidates test, implicit differentiation) are standard AB content and the MVT hypotheses trap is explicitly listed in the reference's 'Common AP traps' section, matching the outline's ap_traps_covered entry. The '5-step optimization method' is a pedagogical scaffold, not a claim about a College-Board-named method, so no citation is required.

decision_rationale: Reviewer A finds the math fully correct (inscribed rectangle, perimeter-40 square, implicit circle tangents all check out) and Reviewer C confirms all topic codes are supported with no fabricated theorems or weightings. Reviewer B raises two minor pedagogical gaps that together cross the >=2 minor threshold: no narrated endpoint check on the [0,2] feasibility interval in the inscribed-rectangle worked example, and the 'misreading f' as f' trap appearing only in the ap_traps_covered metadata list rather than inside the section-06 narration where students actually fall into it. Both are additive narration fixes rather than rewrites, so a light revise pass before script-writing is sufficient.
